title: Goniopteris trifurcata
taxonomy: Goniopteris trifurcata belongs to the division Pteridophyta, which is a group of vascular plants commonly known as ferns. Within this division, it is classified as a member of the class Polypodiopsida. The class Polypodiopsida is further divided into the order Polypodiales, and Goniopteris trifurcata is a part of this order. The family Osmundaceae is where Goniopteris trifurcata is found, and it is classified as a genus of ferns within this family. Goniopteris trifurcata is a species within this genus, characterized by its unique trifurcated fronds.

intro: Goniopteris trifurcata, also known as the Trifurcate Lady Fern or Trifurcate Oak Fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Blechnaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation and protecting against cell damage.
